draft.title: a CRM for freelancers
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a CRM for freelancers

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers need to see deal stages at a glance rather than read detailed notes
facade.label: Client pipeline view
facade.acceptImplies: Show preference for visual workflow states (kanban-style) over narrative-heavy client records
facade.rejectImplies: Show preference for detailed text-based client profiles and conversation history over summary metrics

evidence.hypothesis: Freelancers need to see deal stages at a glance rather than read detailed notes
evidence.implication: Show preference for visual workflow states (kanban-style) over narrative-heavy client records

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers respond positively to soft, inviting interfaces that feel human and approachable rather than corporate or cold
facade.axisTargeted: Color temperature & light quality (warm vs cool)
facade.label: Warm glow
facade.acceptImplies: User leans toward interfaces with golden, amber, or warm-toned color palettes and soft lighting effects
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers cooler tones, stark contrast, or bright clinical aesthetics over warmth and softness

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer CRM interfaces that feel like talking to a friend rather than a corporate software experience
facade.label: Casual and conversational
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into warm, conversational language, personality in microcopy, emoji or playful tone in UI
facade.rejectImplies: Users value efficiency and formal clarity over warmth; pursue clean, business-like interface language instead

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer fluid, animated panel transitions over instant toggles when managing client details and project contexts
facade.label: Smooth slide-out panel
facade.acceptImplies: Explore gestural interactions, easing curves, and transition states as core to the interaction model
facade.rejectImplies: Favor instant state changes, snappy toggles, and minimal motion—keep the interface lean and responsive

facade.hypothesis: Freelance CRM users prefer persistent sidebar access to client/project sections over horizontal tabs, reducing navigation clicks and supporting scanning.
facade.label: Sidebar navigation
facade.acceptImplies: Prioritize left-side persistent navigation for quick section switching; assume heavy multi-tasking between clients.
facade.rejectImplies: Explore top navigation (tabs/breadcrumbs) or card-based browsing; assume tasks are sequential and users stay in one section longer.

facade.hypothesis: A CRM for freelancers benefits from gentle, diffused visual hierarchy rather than harsh contrast—making the interface feel approachable and less corporate.
facade.label: Soft shadows
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into calming, organic lighting metaphors. Soften edges. Prioritize readability over drama. Interface should feel like a trusted notebook, not a command center.
facade.rejectImplies: Users want clarity and immediate visual distinction. Move toward crisp edges, high contrast, and bold primary colors. The tool should feel professional and decisive, not cozy.

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal, professional aesthetics vs. warm, approachable design—reveals if CRM should feel corporate or human-centered
Whether user wants everything visible at once vs. progressive disclosure—reveals if dashboard should be dense or streamlined for freelancers juggling multiple clients
axes.poleA: All at once or step by step?

axes.label: Whether user responds to direct, task-focused language vs. encouraging, motivational tone—reveals if copy should be instructional or supportive
Whether user wants calm, distraction-free environment vs. energetic, dynamic feel—reveals if interface should feel meditative or action-oriented
Whether user prefers numbers and metrics front-and-center vs. narrative summaries—reveals if freelancers want data-heavy dashboards or story-based insights
Whether user expects instant feedback on actions vs. smooth, considered transitions—reveals if CRM should feel snappy and reactive or thoughtful and deliberate

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal, professional aesthetics vs. warm, approachable design—reveals if CRM should feel corporate or human-centered
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants everything visible at once vs. progressive disclosure—reveals if dashboard should be dense or streamlined for freelancers juggling multiple clients
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to direct, task-focused language vs. encouraging, motivational tone—reveals if copy should be instructional or supportive
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants calm, distraction-free environment vs. energetic, dynamic feel—reveals if interface should feel meditative or action-oriented
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers numbers and metrics front-and-center vs. narrative summaries—reveals if freelancers want data-heavy dashboards or story-based insights
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user expects instant feedback on actions vs. smooth, considered transitions—reveals if CRM should feel snappy and reactive or thoughtful and deliberate
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers want financial data surfaced as part of their workflow timeline (mixed with client events) rather than siloed in a separate accounting section
facade.axisTargeted: Integration: scattered modules vs. unified feed
facade.label: Invoice in the feed
facade.acceptImplies: Design leans toward consolidated dashboards where money, milestones, and messages coexist
facade.rejectImplies: Design separates concerns into distinct views (accounting tab, client tab, etc.)—information organized by function, not chronology

draft.summary: A lightweight CRM dashboard for freelancers to track projects, clients, and invoices in one place.
